basics.name: Sergio Márquez
basics.tagline: AI Engineer · AI in production: assistants, smart search and document validation
basics.summary: AI Engineer. I build AI systems that actually run in production: conversational assistants, smart search over documentation, and automated document validation. I work with LLMs, Python/FastAPI and GCP, with a backend, data and operations foundation so AI doesn't stay a demo. Results this past year: document validation from 70% to 90%, ~35% lower infrastructure cost, and ~75% lower data-processing cost. I use Claude Code daily and review with multiple models before critical changes. I measure in metrics, not demos.
basics.headline: I build agentic AI systems in production.

alsoRunning.name: Esem Projects
alsoRunning.description: an applied-AI agency for clinics in Spain

experience.summary: AI Engineer building agentic and RAG systems in production, on top of a GCP data platform and with GitOps practices.
highlights: Multi-tenant conversational assistant with an agentic architecture (orchestrator and sub-agents) on Google ADK, with multi-provider routing via LiteLLM configurable without redeploy.
Automated RAG quality evaluation subsystem (LLM-as-judge, multiple dimensions) with dataset generation at ingestion; fixed a defect that cut false negatives from 91% to nearly 0%.
Document validation pipeline via multi-LLM consensus (GPT + Claude + Gemini) with OCR: validation pass rate from 70% to 90%.

GCP data platform (BigQuery, Dataform, Dataflow; medallion architecture): 3.6M+ records processed with zero data loss, backfill 2× faster (34 → 17 min) and orphan-document cleanup; process cost reduced ~75% (from ~€1,400 to ~€300).
Hybrid search (vector + text + RRF) with reranking and plan-based access control; prompts and models configurable per agent to optimize cost and latency.
Chat migration to an agentic architecture (Google ADK + LiteLLM): ~15% lower latency and ~35% lower infrastructure cost.
AI-assisted development as a daily practice: Claude Code (agents, orchestrators, skills) and multi-model review (Codex/GPT, Gemini) before critical changes.
GitOps on multi-cluster GKE (demo → preprod → prod), GitLab CI/CD with shift-left security (Trivy) and observability (OpenTelemetry/New Relic); 80% minimum coverage as a gate.
Stack: Python, FastAPI, Google ADK, Vertex AI, LiteLLM, BigQuery, Dataform, Dataflow, MongoDB, Firestore, Oracle, GKE, GitLab CI/CD.
experience.headline: Agentic AI systems in production: RAG, LLM evaluation, and a GCP data platform, with real metrics.

experience.summary: Focused on backend specialization (Java/Spring) while owning cloud/data integrations and frontend support.
highlights: Strategic Middleware APIs: Designed and developed Spring-based middleware to optimize frontend/external services communication.
GKE Migration & Optimization: Led the migration to Google Kubernetes Engine, automating continuous delivery and optimizing deployments.
Post-Merger Data Integration: Actively participated in the data integration after the Preving/Cualtis merger, performing mapping and application adaptation.
International Experience (Dublin, 2022): Spent 3 months working remotely from Ireland, improving English fluency and adaptability in global environments.
Stack: Java, Spring Framework, Angular, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, GitLab CI/CD.
experience.headline: Java/Spring backend leading GKE migration and CI/CD.

experience.summary: Intensive training and first contact with professional web development environments.
highlights: Received intensive training in Java Spring, Angular, Docker, and Kubernetes.
Participated in agile development teams, contributing to real-world projects and learning full software lifecycles.
